differential_diagnoses: Duodenal perforation with retroperitoneal abscess and secondary IVC thrombosis
Streptococcus anginosus group liver abscess with portal vein or inferior vena cava involvement
Duodenal-renal fistula (or penetration) due to chronic peptic ulcer disease
Infective endocarditis with septic pulmonary emboli
Antiphospholipid syndrome with secondary venous thrombosis and infection
next_steps: Repeat magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the abdomen with sedation to improve image quality
Upper gastrointestinal endoscopy to evaluate the duodenum
Echocardiogram (transthoracic or transesophageal) to rule out endocarditis
Surgical consultation for potential management of duodenal-renal pathology
Continue targeted antibiotic therapy for Streptococcus anginosus and optimize anticoagulation
Serial monitoring of abdominal physical examination and inflammatory markers

differential_diagnoses: Penetrating duodenal ulcer causing a retroperitoneal abscess and secondary venous thrombosis
Streptococcus anginosus group infection with septic thrombophlebitis (pylephlebitis or IVC thrombosis)
Strongyloides stercoralis hyperinfection syndrome with secondary bacterial translocation
Biliary source of sepsis with periportal inflammatory involvement
next_steps: Transesophageal echocardiography to rule out infective endocarditis
Upper gastrointestinal endoscopy to evaluate the duodenum and rule out perforation/fistula
Surgical consultation for management of potential retroperitoneal/duodenal pathology
Strongyloides serology and stool examination for parasites
Magnetic resonance cholangiopancreatography (MRCP) to further evaluate biliary/periportal findings
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics and therapeutic anticoagulation
